description: Use this skill whenever the user wants a unified design-spec audit — either detecting inconsistencies BETWEEN design documents under `DESIGN/` (type / field drift, divergent API contracts, dependency cycles, DB-schema disagreements, terminology and constant drift) OR comparing design documents AGAINST the implementation (Missing / Diverged / Extra / Constraint). Trigger phrases include "audit our design docs", "do the spec markdowns line up", "API contract drift across designs", "scan DESIGN/*.md for mismatches" (cross-spec mode), as well as "check spec vs implementation", "does the code match DESIGN/01_auth.md?", "verify implementation matches the spec", "list missing items from the design doc", "find diverged signatures between spec and code" (conformance mode). Trigger even when the user does not say "spec-audit" — implicit phrases like "did the design docs drift?", "is the implementation behind the spec?", "anything off between these markdowns?", or "compare what we built against what we designed" qualify.

results.query: Just added DESIGN/03_payment.md and DESIGN/04_refund.md. Could you check if the field names align with the existing 5 design docs? I'm worried about TransactionId vs transaction_id inconsistency.

I've updated 3 of our component design docs (DESIGN/02_user.md, DESIGN/04_order.md, DESIGN/06_inventory.md) to add the new pagination spec. Before I open the PR, can you make sure none of the API contracts have drifted across docs?

We have 8 markdown files under DESIGN/ and I keep finding little inconsistencies between them when I read implementation. Can you do a sweep across the design docs and surface anything that looks off?
yo can u sanity check the design docs in DESIGN/ for me, especially the type names. been refactoring all afternoon and im seeing UserId vs user_id all over the place
Run a cross-document audit on our spec markdowns under DESIGN/ — I want to catch type name variations, field mismatches, and any circular dependencies before the freeze tomorrow morning.
I want to verify type names are consistent between DESIGN/01_user.md and DESIGN/02_order.md. Things like UserId vs user_id will bite us in the implementation phase later.

Editing the auth component spec right now, but I'm worried about constant drift across components. CONST_TIMEOUT might have different values between DESIGN/auth.md and the other yaml configs we have under DESIGN/configs/.
wanna do a one-shot check on the spec markdowns in DESIGN/, anything misaligned in field names? been touching all of them last week and lost track
Audit the 6 design docs we just generated from design-phase as a batch. I want type, API contract, and DB schema inconsistencies surfaced before review.

We have a constant CONNECT_TIMEOUT_MS defined in DESIGN/01_gateway.md and DESIGN/05_worker.md but I'm seeing different values (3000 vs 5000). Can you sweep all design docs for this kind of constant mismatch?
Compare my implementation in src/auth/ against DESIGN/01_auth.md and tell me if anything is missing or diverged from the spec.
I have a list of 12 spec mismatches from the last spec-check run, can you fix the implementation to match the design?
Generate a brand new design document for the payment retry workflow, we don't have one yet — start from plans/PAYMENT-RETRY.md.
Audit the security logs from last month for unauthorized access attempts to /admin endpoints. The logs are in /var/log/access.log
Rename the variable getUserData to fetchUser across the codebase, including all .ts and .tsx files.
Verify whether the src/order/ implementation matches DESIGN/03_order.md — list out Missing items and Diverged fields between the spec and the actual code.
Deep security review on the auth code under src/auth/ — focus on injection vectors and authorization logic, give me S-Critical and S-High findings.
I want to implement an audit log feature and need a spec for the log format. How should I structure it for ELK ingestion?
Update the dependencies in package.json to their latest versions — npm outdated shows several upgradable packages including react and zod.
Write DESIGN/04_payment.md from scratch for the new payment component, ignore the existing docs for now.
